<!--
Please have patience, we usually need a few weeks to get around to reviewing your submission.
To help us do so, please provide some information about your plugin:
-->

## Plugin Information

**Plugin Name:** [Your plugin name]

**Plugin ID:** [kebab-case-id, e.g., my-awesome-plugin]

**Repository URL:** [Your GitHub repository URL]

**Description:** [Brief description of what your plugin does]

## Checklist

- [ ] I'm the plugin's author and/or maintainer.
- [ ] I have read the [CONTRIBUTING.md](../CONTRIBUTING.md) guide.
- [ ] My plugin repository has a tagged release with a [semver](https://semver.org) version number (e.g., v1.0.0).
- [ ] My plugin repository has a README describing what it's for and how to use it.
- [ ] My plugin compiles to a WASM file with the required `handle_url` export.
- [ ] I have added my plugin entry to `repository/index.json` in alphabetical order.
- [ ] I have created the plugin metadata file in `repository/{first-letter}/{plugin-id}.json`.
- [ ] The WASM file URL in my plugin metadata points to a release asset in my repository.
- [ ] I have included the SHA256 hash of the WASM file in the metadata.
- [ ] I have specified the required permissions for my plugin.
- [ ] My plugin follows the Browseraptor Plugin API specification.

## What does your plugin do?

[Describe what your plugin does, what URLs it handles, and what actions it performs]

## Why should this plugin be added?

[Explain why this plugin is useful and should be included in the default channel]

## Similar plugins

There are no plugins like it in Browseraptor.
<!-- OR -->
My plugin is similar to [similar-plugin-name]. However it should still be added because [explain why it's different or better].

<!--
A word about AI use:
Although we use automation, a human will be reviewing your submission. An important part of this is an assessment of the ability and willingness of the human submitting the plugin (i.e. you) to support it long term. This is therefore primarily a human to human conversation. While you're welcome to use any form of automation, you are expected to participate in this conversation yourself.
-->
